description: Woven or felted material; fabric for covering, clothing, or use. 'Wetha' names what covers and protects—threads become shelter.
sound_symbolism: The word weaves softly. 'We' interlaces in relationship, threads meeting. Then 'tha' breathes gentle texture, the soft dental fricative evoking the lightness of fabric against skin.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo wetha' (the cloth). General term for woven material. Related to 'liru' (fabric) and 'sima' (thread).

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cloth sustained life—warmth, shelter, identity woven thread by thread.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Covering without attachment—cloth protects the body, reminds of impermanence.
